Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy

Welcome to ICCMA 2026
æ¨çæµè§å¨ä¸æ¯æè§é¢æ ç­¾ã
ICCMA2025-Review
Control, Mechatronics and Automation is an interdisciplinary field that combines control engineering, mechanical engineering, and electronic engineering. In recent years, advancements in artificial intelligence, internet of things (IoT), and big data analytics have been driving the development of this field. Control, Mechatronics and Automation has become increasingly important in modern manufacturing and production systems, as well as in robotics, aerospace, transportation, and medical industries. The field is rapidly evolving with new advances in sensors, actuators, control algorithms, and communication technologies.
Since its inception in 2013, ICCMA has served as a key platform for international academic exchange. Previous editions of the conference have been successfully held in Sydney (Australia), Dubai (UAE), Barcelona (Spain), Edmonton (Canada), Tokyo (Japan), Moscow (Russia), and Luxembourg.In 2022, ICCMA was organized in a hybrid format, combining both on-site and online participation. The conference continued its global journey in 2023 in Norway, followed by ICCMA 2024 in London, UK. Most recently, the 13th ICCMA was held in Paris, France, in 2025, where it achieved great success and received widespread recognition from participants worldwide.
Based on the success of that, 2026 14th International Conference on Control, Mechatronics and Automation (ICCMA)
will be held at
Bari, Italy
, from
November 3-5, 2026
. It provides a platform for researchers, scholars, engineers, and students from around the world to present their research, share ideas, and foster research collaborations between academia and industry. ICCMA is a well-known event worldwide and has been attracting increasing numbers of paper submissions and attendees every year.
The conference is sponsored by Polytechnic University of Bari, Italy.
Publication
Submitted papers will be peer reviewed and accepted papers after proper registration will be published in the
conference proceedings
.

>>Academic Ethics:
Academic integrity is the moral code or ethical policy of academia. This includes values such as avoidance of cheating or plagiarism; maintenance of academic standards; honesty and rigor in research and academic publishing. Each scholar should obey the rules. If not, the committee have the right to reject the paper directly when review the papers. Due to the update of database, but the publication process is long, if the press found the review paper is with plagiarism or content with high repetition rate, the press can reject the paper publication directly. The author will undertake all the responsibility.
Submission
The purpose of the Conference is to provide a common forum for researchers, scientists, and students from all over the world to present their latest research findings, ideas, developments and applications in the broader areas of Control, Mechatronics and Automation.
Please submit your full paper to
Easy Chair System
(.pdf only)
Please download the
Template(word)
|
Template(Latex)
to edit your paper submission before you submit your full paper.
The minimum page limit for full paper is
4
pages including all figures, tables, and references.
5
pages is the standard length. When it exceeds
6
pages, each additional page will be chargeable.
English is the official language; and the paper should be written and presented only in English.
Abstract will be accepted and author will be invited to give the oral presentation as well, however, neither the abstract nor the presentation would be published.
All submissions will undergo a double-blind peer review process.
Reviewers will evaluate the paper based on originality, technical quality, and relevance to the conference themes.
Special Sessions at ICCMA 2026
The 14th International Conference on Control, Mechatronics and Automation (ICCMA 2026) is proud to present a series of Special Sessions that highlight the latest advancements and emerging trends in the fields of control, mechatronics, robotics, and automation. These sessions are designed to foster collaboration and showcase cutting-edge research addressing the challenges and opportunities in todayâs rapidly evolving technological landscape. Welcome to join us to organize a special session which falls into your research area.
Special Session No. | Session Title (more to be updated, click for details...)
Session 1 | Nonlinear and Fractional-Order Control for Complex Mechatronic Systems | Organizer(s): TBA
Session 2 | Smart Automation and IoT-Enabled Industrial Process Control | Organizer(s): TBA
Session 3 | Machine Learning for Biomedical Instrumentation and Control | Organizer(s): TBA
Session 4 | Flexible Manufacturing and Additive Manufacturing for Industry 4.0 | Organizer(s): TBA
Session 5 | Vibration Control and Dynamics of Flexible Systems in Robotics | Organizer(s): TBA
Event History
ICCMA is initiated in Sydney (Australia) in 2013, followed by Dubai (UAE) in 2014 and the two consecutive editions of 2015 and 2016 in Barcelona (Spain), 2017 at University of Alberta, Edmonton, Canada, 2018 in Tokyo, Japan, 2019 in TU Delft, the Netherlands, 2020 in Moscow, Russia(Virtual), 2021 in Luxembourg(Virtual), and 2022 in Luxembourg (Hybrid), 2023 in University of Agder, Norway, 2024 in Brunel University London, UK, 2025 in University of Versailles, France. ICCMA has the good indexing history of EI Compendex and Scopus.

=== PAGE: https://www.iccma.org/visa.html ===
Link: Visa
Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
VISA
Schengen Visa Guidelines for Italy
For participants attending the conference from outside the Schengen Area, a Schengen visa may be required to enter Italy. Below are the general steps and requirements for applying for a Schengen visa:
Visa Type
: Participants attending the conference will need to apply for a
Short-Stay Schengen Visa
(Type C), which allows you to stay in Italy and other Schengen countries for up to 90 days for business or conference purposes.
Required Documents
:
A completed and signed visa application form.
A valid passport (with at least two blank pages and valid for at least three months beyond the planned departure date).
Recent passport-sized photographs (compliant with ICAO standards).
Invitation Letter
: An official confirmation letter from the conference organizers in Italy.
Proof of travel insurance (minimum coverage of â¬30,000 for medical expenses and repatriation in the Schengen Area).
Proof of accommodation in Italy (hotel booking or declaration of hospitality).
Round-trip flight reservation/itinerary.
Financial proof (recent bank statements or evidence of sponsorship) to show sufficient funds for the stay.
Visa Application Process
:
Submit your application at the nearest Italian Embassy, Consulate, or authorized visa service center (such as VFS Global or CKGS) in your country.
Book an appointment well in advance, as slots can be limited during peak seasons.
Attend the interview (if required) and provide biometric data (fingerprints).
Pay the visa application fee (standard fee is â¬90, though exemptions may apply for certain categories).
Processing Time
: It typically takes about 15 calendar days to process an Italian Schengen visa. However, we strongly recommend applying at least
4â6 weeks
before your intended travel date to account for any delays.
Important Notes
:
If Italy is your main destination or the first point of entry into the Schengen Area, you must apply through the Italian diplomatic mission.
Ensure all documents are in English or Italian (or accompanied by a certified translation if required by the specific consulate).
The consular office reserves the right to request additional documentation or a personal interview.
Copyright Â© 2026 The 14th 
					International Conference on Control, Mechatronics and 
					Automation (ICCMA 2026)

=== PAGE: https://www.iccma.org/speaker.html ===
Link: Keynote Speakers
Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
Keynote Speakers
Prof. Maria Pia Fanti, IEEE FELLOW
Polytechnic of Bari, Italy
Maria Pia Fanti is full professor of System and Control Engineering at the Department of Electrical and Information Engineering of the Polytechnic of Bari (Italy). She received the Laurea degree in Electronic Engineering from the University of Pisa (Italy), in 1983. She was a visiting researcher at the Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute of Troy, New York, in 1999. Since 1983 she has been with the Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering of the Polytechnic of Bari (Italy), where she was Assistant Professor from 1990 till 1998 and Associate Professor from 1990 till April 2012.
Speech Title: Artificial Intelligence Approaches for Cooperative, Connected and Automated Mobility
Abstract: Cooperative, Connected and Automated Mobility (CCAM) is expected to reshape the way of travelling and moving in Europe and around the world. By the CCAM, the automated vehicles have to be integrated into the mobility and transport system by designing and implementing infrastructures, new services, platforms, cooperation and governance models.
Prof. Shane Xie, IEEE Fellow
University of Leeds, UK
Prof Shane (Sheng Q) Xie, Ph.D., FRSNZ, FEngNZ, FIEEE, FASME, FIMechE and FAAIA, is the Chair of Robotics and Autonomous Systems and Director of the Rehabilitation Robotics Lab at the University of Leeds. He has >30 years of research experience in healthcare robotics and exoskeletons. He has published > 500 refereed papers and 8 books in rehabilitation exoskeleton design and control, neuromuscular modelling, and advanced human-robot interaction.
Prof. Jing Zhou
University of Agder, Norway
Jing Zhou is a Professor at the Faculty of Engineering and Science, University of Agder, Norway. She is the leader of Robotics and Automation group, and the research director of Priority Research Center of Mechatronics. Prior to joining the University of Agder in 2016, she was a senior research scientist at International Research Institute of Stavanger in Norway from 2009 to 2016.

Link: ICCMA2025
Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA 2025 | NOV. 24-26 | 
								University of Versailles, France
The 13th International Conference on Control, Mechatronics and Automation (ICCMA 2025) was successfully held in Paris, France, at the University of Versailles. The conference attracted more than 150 participants from over 30 countries and regions, including Europe, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East, highlighting ICCMAâs strong international appeal and growing global influence.
The technical program of ICCMA 2025 was carefully structured to reflect current research trends and emerging challenges. The conference featured two main technical tracks, nine parallel oral sessions, two poster sessions, and dedicated online presentation sessions, ensuring broad participation and flexibility. In total, 110 high-quality papers were accepted and presented, covering a wide range of topics from intelligent control and robotics to automation systems and cyber-physical technologies. In addition, eight distinguished keynote and invited speakers delivered insightful talks, providing valuable perspectives on the future development of the field and stimulating active discussions among participants.
The opening session was chaired by Prof. Amar Ramdane-Cherif (University of Versailles Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines, France). The conference officially commenced with opening remarks by Prof. Laurent Dumas (University of Versailles, France), followed by a welcome address delivered by Prof. Belkacem Ould Bouamama (CRIStAL Laboratory, Polytech Lille, France), setting a strong academic tone for the event.
The plenary program featured two distinguished keynote speeches. Prof. Maria Pia Fanti, IEEE Fellow (Polytechnic University of Bari, Italy). Prof. Jing Zhou (University of Agder, Norway) delivered the second keynote . In addition, several invited talks further enriched the technical content of the conference. Prof. Roland BÃ¼chi (Zurich University of Applied Sciences, Switzerland) , Prof. Saulo H. Freitas Seabra da Rocha (Ruhr West University of Applied Sciences, Germany) . Prof. Jan DÃ¼nnweber (Ostbayerische Technische Hochschule Regensburg, Germany), Prof. Stijn Derammelaere (University of Antwerp, Belgium), and Assoc. Prof. Lorenzo Scalera (University of Udine, Italy) .
ICCMA 2025 was co-sponsored by the University of Versailles and the University of Lille. The organizers sincerely acknowledge their generous support and excellent local arrangements. Appreciation is also extended to the organizing committee, technical committee members, reviewers, and volunteers for their professionalism and dedication, which were essential to the success of the conference.

Link: Organizer
Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
Organizer
The Polytechnic University of Bari is one of the Italian universities with a scientific-technological character recognized as a center of excellence for education and research in the fields of engineering, architecture, and industrial design. Poliba stands out for its strong connection with the territory and its commitment to sustainable development and technological innovation. The university promotes a teaching approach oriented towards practice and collaboration with local, national, and international businesses and institutions.
It trains, according to high-quality standards, professionals in the fields of engineering, architecture, design, and territorial planning, awarding academic degrees of university diploma and degree (old system, being phased out), bachelor's degree, master's degree, doctoral degree, with the corresponding academic qualifications, as well as first and second level university master's degrees.
The University of Lille (French: UniversitÃ© de Lille, abbreviated as ULille, UDL or univ-lille) is a French public research university based in Lille, Hauts-de-France. It has its origins in the University of Douai (1559), and resulted from the merger of three universities â Lille 1 University of Science and Technology, Lille 2 University of Health and Law, and Charles de Gaulle University â Lille III in 2018. With more than 80,000 students, it is one of the largest universities in France and one of the largest French-speaking universities in the world.
Since 2017, the university has been funded as one of the French universities of excellence. It benefits from an endowment of 500 million euros to accelerate its strategy in education, research, international development and outreach.
With 66 research labs, 350 PhD theses supported per year and 3,000 scientific publications each year, it is well represented in the research community; it collaborates with many organizations (Pasteur Institute of Lille, CHU Lille University Hospital, CNRS, INSERM, INRA, INRIA etc.) and schools (Ãcole Centrale de Lille, Ãcole des Mines-TÃ©lÃ©com de Lille-Douai (IMT Lille Douai), Sciences Po Lille etc.).
Until 2019, the university was the main component of the Community of Universities and Institutions (COMUE) Lille Nord de France. It still operates the European Doctoral College, which federates universities and other higher learning institutes in the Hauts-de-France region.
University of Versailles Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines (French: UniversitÃ© de Versailles Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines, pronounced is a French public university created in 1991, located in the department of Yvelines and, since 2002, in Hauts-de-Seine. It is a constituent university of the federal Paris-Saclay University.
Consisting of eight separate campuses, it is mainly located in the cities of Versailles, Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines, Mantes-en-Yvelines, VÃ©lizy-Villacoublay and Rambouillet. It is one of the five universities of the Academy of Versailles.[n 1]
It is one of the four universitÃ©s nouvelles (new universities)[n 2] inaugurated in the Ãle-de-France region after the 2000 University project (French: plan universitÃ© 2000). It has a population of 19,000 students, a staff of 752 people, and 1,389 teachers and researchers, as well as an additional 285 external teachers.
The university teaches courses in the fields of natural science, social science, political science, engineering, technology, and medicine. It also provides interdisciplinary courses covering the relationships across economics, ethics, natural environment and sustainable development.

Link: Topics
Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
CONFERENCE SCOPE
The conference aims to bring together 
										the two fields of system design and 
										control together. Today, flexible 
										robotics and compliant mechanism design 
										use nonlinearities which were once 
										considered hindrances to system design 
										to their advantage. In addition, 
										advances in control technology such as 
										nonlinear control, fractional order 
										control are advantageously being used to 
										go beyond classical linear control and 
										therefore increase the applicability of 
										complex mechanisms for robotics and 
										mechatronic systems. In this regard, the 
										14th International Conference in Control, 
										Mechatronics and Automation solicits 
										original articles in the field of: (But not only limited for the 
										below 
										topics.)
Track 1: Control Theory and ApplicationsAdaptive controlCo-operative controlDelay systemsDiscrete event systemsFuzzy systemsNonlinear control for motion systemsRobust control | Track 2: Mechatronics and RoboticsCompliant mechanismFlexible Manufacturing Technology & SystemFlexible robotics, Soft roboticsHigh-speed/precision machiningLaser Processing TechnologyMechatronic system designNano-scale automation and assemblyPrecision Manufacturing & MeasurementPrecision Mechanics & MechatronicsPrecision mechanismPrecision motion controlSmart automation

It is our distinct pleasure to welcome you all to the 11th International Conference on Control, Mechatronics, and Automation. This remarkable gathering of minds from around the world, in the pursuit of advancing the fields of control, mechatronics, and automation, is a testament to the enduring spirit of innovation and collaboration that defines our global community.
In recent years, advancements in artificial intelligence, internet of things (IoT), and big data analytics have been driving the development of this field. Control, Mechatronics and Automation has become increasingly important in modern manufacturing and production systems, as well as in robotics, aerospace, transportation, and medical industries. The field is rapidly evolving with new advances in sensors, actuators, control algorithms, and communication technologies.
This conference is more than just a meeting of great minds; it is a nexus of inspiration, creativity, and collaboration. It's where ideas are conceived, knowledge is transferred, and partnerships are forged. The discussions and discoveries made here will undoubtedly chart the course for the future of these fields.

Link: FAQs
Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
Frequently Asked 
										Questions
How to submit?
1. You need to edit your paper as instructed by 
								the template.
2. Send your paper to the
Easy 
								Chair System
.
3. After receiving your submission, the 
								conference secretary will contact you within 2 
								workdays and assign you a PAPER ID.
4. If 
								paper publication is your goal, then you can 
								submit full paper directly, no need to submit 
								abstract separately.
How long 
									is the paper review?
For each paper, it will take around 20-30 days 
								for review. You will receive feedback email.
1) For Paper receiving (2-3 workdays after your 
								successful submission)
2) Preliminary feedback (around 10-15 days after 
								your successful submission)
3) Acceptance letter notification (around 10-15 
								days after preliminary feedback)
What is the acceptance rate?
Each paper will be reviewed based on template, 
								topic, novelty, introduction, conceptual 
								quality, methodological quality, results, 
								discussion, readability, etc. The acceptance rate for our conference typically depends on the number and quality of submitted papers. The exact rate may vary from year to year, and we strive to maintain a high standard of quality for the presentations and discussions. We encourage authors to submit their best work, as the review process is highly competitive.
How to know whether my paper topic related to 
									the conference theme or not?
Please check the call for paper page:
http://iccma.org/topics.html
If your research topic is included here, it will 
								be good. If not, you also can submit your paper. 
								The technical committee will review the paper 
								topic firstly,you will know if your paper topics 
								related or not. Generally, you can know the 
								result within one week, even it is not related 
								to our conference, we can recommend you some 
								other related conferences.
How to make registration and how much should I pay?
Firstly, you need to figure out your 
								registration type: author (full paper 
								submission), presentation only (abstract 
								submission), listener (without paper or abstract 
								submission). Then you can check the detail 
								registration fee on our registration page:
http://iccma.org/reg.html
Is it necessary to register after submission?
You need to register after receiving the 
								notification of acceptance. We will inform you 
								the registration information by email when your 
								paper passes the review process.

					International Conference on Control, Mechatronics and 
					Automation (ICCMA 2026)

ICCMA2025-Review
Control, Mechatronics and Automation is an interdisciplinary field that combines control engineering, mechanical engineering, and electronic engineering. In recent years, advancements in artificial intelligence, internet of things (IoT), and big data analytics have been driving the development of this field. Control, Mechatronics and Automation has become increasingly important in modern manufacturing and production systems, as well as in robotics, aerospace, transportation, and medical industries. The field is rapidly evolving with new advances in sensors, actuators, control algorithms, and communication technologies.
Since its inception in 2013, ICCMA has served as a key platform for international academic exchange. Previous editions of the conference have been successfully held in Sydney (Australia), Dubai (UAE), Barcelona (Spain), Edmonton (Canada), Tokyo (Japan), Moscow (Russia), and Luxembourg.In 2022, ICCMA was organized in a hybrid format, combining both on-site and online participation. The conference continued its global journey in 2023 in Norway, followed by ICCMA 2024 in London, UK. Most recently, the 13th ICCMA was held in Paris, France, in 2025, where it achieved great success and received widespread recognition from participants worldwide.
Based on the success of that, 2026 14th International Conference on Control, Mechatronics and Automation (ICCMA)
will be held at
Bari, Italy
, from
November 3-5, 2026
. It provides a platform for researchers, scholars, engineers, and students from around the world to present their research, share ideas, and foster research collaborations between academia and industry. ICCMA is a well-known event worldwide and has been attracting increasing numbers of paper submissions and attendees every year.
The conference is sponsored by Polytechnic University of Bari, Italy.
Publication
Submitted papers will be peer reviewed and accepted papers after proper registration will be published in the
conference proceedings
.

>>Academic Ethics:
Academic integrity is the moral code or ethical policy of academia. This includes values such as avoidance of cheating or plagiarism; maintenance of academic standards; honesty and rigor in research and academic publishing. Each scholar should obey the rules. If not, the committee have the right to reject the paper directly when review the papers. Due to the update of database, but the publication process is long, if the press found the review paper is with plagiarism or content with high repetition rate, the press can reject the paper publication directly. The author will undertake all the responsibility.
Submission
The purpose of the Conference is to provide a common forum for researchers, scientists, and students from all over the world to present their latest research findings, ideas, developments and applications in the broader areas of Control, Mechatronics and Automation.
Please submit your full paper to
Easy Chair System
(.pdf only)
Please download the
Template(word)
|
Template(Latex)
to edit your paper submission before you submit your full paper.
The minimum page limit for full paper is
4
pages including all figures, tables, and references.
5
pages is the standard length. When it exceeds
6
pages, each additional page will be chargeable.
English is the official language; and the paper should be written and presented only in English.
Abstract will be accepted and author will be invited to give the oral presentation as well, however, neither the abstract nor the presentation would be published.
All submissions will undergo a double-blind peer review process.
Reviewers will evaluate the paper based on originality, technical quality, and relevance to the conference themes.

The 14th International Conference on Control, Mechatronics and Automation (ICCMA 2026) is proud to present a series of Special Sessions that highlight the latest advancements and emerging trends in the fields of control, mechatronics, robotics, and automation. These sessions are designed to foster collaboration and showcase cutting-edge research addressing the challenges and opportunities in todayâs rapidly evolving technological landscape. Link: Keynote Speakers
Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
Keynote Speakers
Prof. Maria Pia Fanti, IEEE FELLOW
Polytechnic of Bari, Italy
Maria Pia Fanti is full professor of System and Control Engineering at the Department of Electrical and Information Engineering of the Polytechnic of Bari (Italy). She received the Laurea degree in Electronic Engineering from the University of Pisa (Italy), in 1983. She was a visiting researcher at the Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute of Troy, New York, in 1999. Since 1983 she has been with the Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering of the Polytechnic of Bari (Italy), where she was Assistant Professor from 1990 till 1998 and Associate Professor from 1990 till April 2012.
Speech Title: Artificial Intelligence Approaches for Cooperative, Connected and Automated Mobility
Abstract: Cooperative, Connected and Automated Mobility (CCAM) is expected to reshape the way of travelling and moving in Europe and around the world. By the CCAM, the automated vehicles have to be integrated into the mobility and transport system by designing and implementing infrastructures, new services, platforms, cooperation and governance models.
Prof. Shane Xie, IEEE Fellow
University of Leeds, UK
Prof Shane (Sheng Q) Xie, Ph.D., FRSNZ, FEngNZ, FIEEE, FASME, FIMechE and FAAIA, is the Chair of Robotics and Autonomous Systems and Director of the Rehabilitation Robotics Lab at the University of Leeds. He has >30 years of research experience in healthcare robotics and exoskeletons. He has published > 500 refereed papers and 8 books in rehabilitation exoskeleton design and control, neuromuscular modelling, and advanced human-robot interaction.
Prof. Jing Zhou
University of Agder, Norway
Jing Zhou is a Professor at the Faculty of Engineering and Science, University of Agder, Norway. She is the leader of Robotics and Automation group, and the research director of Priority Research Center of Mechatronics. Prior to joining the University of Agder in 2016, she was a senior research scientist at International Research Institute of Stavanger in Norway from 2009 to 2016.

ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
Organizer
The Polytechnic University of Bari is one of the Italian universities with a scientific-technological character recognized as a center of excellence for education and research in the fields of engineering, architecture, and industrial design. Poliba stands out for its strong connection with the territory and its commitment to sustainable development and technological innovation. The university promotes a teaching approach oriented towards practice and collaboration with local, national, and international businesses and institutions.
It trains, according to high-quality standards, professionals in the fields of engineering, architecture, design, and territorial planning, awarding academic degrees of university diploma and degree (old system, being phased out), bachelor's degree, master's degree, doctoral degree, with the corresponding academic qualifications, as well as first and second level university master's degrees.
The University of Lille (French: UniversitÃ© de Lille, abbreviated as ULille, UDL or univ-lille) is a French public research university based in Lille, Hauts-de-France. It has its origins in the University of Douai (1559), and resulted from the merger of three universities â Lille 1 University of Science and Technology, Lille 2 University of Health and Law, and Charles de Gaulle University â Lille III in 2018. With more than 80,000 students, it is one of the largest universities in France and one of the largest French-speaking universities in the world.
Since 2017, the university has been funded as one of the French universities of excellence. It benefits from an endowment of 500 million euros to accelerate its strategy in education, research, international development and outreach.
With 66 research labs, 350 PhD theses supported per year and 3,000 scientific publications each year, it is well represented in the research community; it collaborates with many organizations (Pasteur Institute of Lille, CHU Lille University Hospital, CNRS, INSERM, INRA, INRIA etc.) and schools (Ãcole Centrale de Lille, Ãcole des Mines-TÃ©lÃ©com de Lille-Douai (IMT Lille Douai), Sciences Po Lille etc.).
Until 2019, the university was the main component of the Community of Universities and Institutions (COMUE) Lille Nord de France. It still operates the European Doctoral College, which federates universities and other higher learning institutes in the Hauts-de-France region.
University of Versailles Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines (French: UniversitÃ© de Versailles Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines, pronounced is a French public university created in 1991, located in the department of Yvelines and, since 2002, in Hauts-de-Seine. It is a constituent university of the federal Paris-Saclay University.
Consisting of eight separate campuses, it is mainly located in the cities of Versailles, Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines, Mantes-en-Yvelines, VÃ©lizy-Villacoublay and Rambouillet. It is one of the five universities of the Academy of Versailles.[n 1]
It is one of the four universitÃ©s nouvelles (new universities)[n 2] inaugurated in the Ãle-de-France region after the 2000 University project (French: plan universitÃ© 2000). It has a population of 19,000 students, a staff of 752 people, and 1,389 teachers and researchers, as well as an additional 285 external teachers.
The university teaches courses in the fields of natural science, social science, political science, engineering, technology, and medicine. It also provides interdisciplinary courses covering the relationships across economics, ethics, natural environment and sustainable development.

CONFERENCE SCOPE
The conference aims to bring together 
										the two fields of system design and 
										control together. Today, flexible 
										robotics and compliant mechanism design 
										use nonlinearities which were once 
										considered hindrances to system design 
										to their advantage. In addition, 
										advances in control technology such as 
										nonlinear control, fractional order 
										control are advantageously being used to 
										go beyond classical linear control and 
										therefore increase the applicability of 
										complex mechanisms for robotics and 
										mechatronic systems. In this regard, the 
										14th International Conference in Control, 
										Mechatronics and Automation solicits

Link: ICCMA2025
Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA 2025 | NOV. 24-26 | 
								University of Versailles, France
The 13th International Conference on Control, Mechatronics and Automation (ICCMA 2025) was successfully held in Paris, France, at the University of Versailles. The conference attracted more than 150 participants from over 30 countries and regions, including Europe, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East, highlighting ICCMAâs strong international appeal and growing global influence.
The technical program of ICCMA 2025 was carefully structured to reflect current research trends and emerging challenges. The conference featured two main technical tracks, nine parallel oral sessions, two poster sessions, and dedicated online presentation sessions, ensuring broad participation and flexibility. In total, 110 high-quality papers were accepted and presented, covering a wide range of topics from intelligent control and robotics to automation systems and cyber-physical technologies. In addition, eight distinguished keynote and invited speakers delivered insightful talks, providing valuable perspectives on the future development of the field and stimulating active discussions among participants.
The opening session was chaired by Prof. Amar Ramdane-Cherif (University of Versailles Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines, France). The conference officially commenced with opening remarks by Prof. Laurent Dumas (University of Versailles, France), followed by a welcome address delivered by Prof. Belkacem Ould Bouamama (CRIStAL Laboratory, Polytech Lille, France), setting a strong academic tone for the event.
The plenary program featured two distinguished keynote speeches. Prof. Maria Pia Fanti, IEEE Fellow (Polytechnic University of Bari, Italy). Prof. Jing Zhou (University of Agder, Norway) delivered the second keynote . In addition, several invited talks further enriched the technical content of the conference. Prof. Roland BÃ¼chi (Zurich University of Applied Sciences, Switzerland) , Prof. Saulo H. Freitas Seabra da Rocha (Ruhr West University of Applied Sciences, Germany) . Prof. Jan DÃ¼nnweber (Ostbayerische Technische Hochschule Regensburg, Germany), Prof. Stijn Derammelaere (University of Antwerp, Belgium), and Assoc. Prof. Lorenzo Scalera (University of Udine, Italy) .
ICCMA 2025 was co-sponsored by the University of Versailles and the University of Lille. The organizers sincerely acknowledge their generous support and excellent local arrangements. Appreciation is also extended to the organizing committee, technical committee members, reviewers, and volunteers for their professionalism and dedication, which were essential to the success of the conference.

It is our distinct pleasure to welcome you all to the 11th International Conference on Control, Mechatronics, and Automation. This remarkable gathering of minds from around the world, in the pursuit of advancing the fields of control, mechatronics, and automation, is a testament to the enduring spirit of innovation and collaboration that defines our global community.
In recent years, advancements in artificial intelligence, internet of things (IoT), and big data analytics have been driving the development of this field. Control, Mechatronics and Automation has become increasingly important in modern manufacturing and production systems, as well as in robotics, aerospace, transportation, and medical industries. The field is rapidly evolving with new advances in sensors, actuators, control algorithms, and communication technologies.
This conference is more than just a meeting of great minds; it is a nexus of inspiration, creativity, and collaboration. It's where ideas are conceived, knowledge is transferred, and partnerships are forged. The discussions and discoveries made here will undoubtedly chart the course for the future of these fields.

Link: FAQs
Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
Frequently Asked 
										Questions
How to submit?
1. You need to edit your paper as instructed by 
								the template.
2. Send your paper to the
Easy 
								Chair System
.
3. After receiving your submission, the 
								conference secretary will contact you within 2 
								workdays and assign you a PAPER ID.
4. If 
								paper publication is your goal, then you can 
								submit full paper directly, no need to submit 
								abstract separately.
How long 
									is the paper review?
For each paper, it will take around 20-30 days 
								for review. You will receive feedback email.
1) For Paper receiving (2-3 workdays after your 
								successful submission)
2) Preliminary feedback (around 10-15 days after 
								your successful submission)
3) Acceptance letter notification (around 10-15 
								days after preliminary feedback)
What is the acceptance rate?
Each paper will be reviewed based on template, 
								topic, novelty, introduction, conceptual 
								quality, methodological quality, results, 
								discussion, readability, etc. The acceptance rate for our conference typically depends on the number and quality of submitted papers. The exact rate may vary from year to year, and we strive to maintain a high standard of quality for the presentations and discussions. We encourage authors to submit their best work, as the review process is highly competitive.
How to know whether my paper topic related to 
									the conference theme or not?
Please check the call for paper page:
http://iccma.org/topics.html
If your research topic is included here, it will 
								be good. If not, you also can submit your paper. 
								The technical committee will review the paper 
								topic firstly,you will know if your paper topics 
								related or not. Generally, you can know the 
								result within one week, even it is not related 
								to our conference, we can recommend you some 
								other related conferences.
How to make registration and how much should I pay?
Firstly, you need to figure out your 
								registration type: author (full paper 
								submission), presentation only (abstract 
								submission), listener (without paper or abstract 
								submission). Then you can check the detail 
								registration fee on our registration page:
http://iccma.org/reg.html
Is it necessary to register after submission?
You need to register after receiving the 
								notification of acceptance. We will inform you 
								the registration information by email when your 
								paper passes the review process.

Link: Visa
Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
VISA
Schengen Visa Guidelines for Italy
For participants attending the conference from outside the Schengen Area, a Schengen visa may be required to enter Italy. Below are the general steps and requirements for applying for a Schengen visa:
Visa Type
: Participants attending the conference will need to apply for a
Short-Stay Schengen Visa
(Type C), which allows you to stay in Italy and other Schengen countries for up to 90 days for business or conference purposes.
Required Documents
:
A completed and signed visa application form.
A valid passport (with at least two blank pages and valid for at least three months beyond the planned departure date).
Recent passport-sized photographs (compliant with ICAO standards).
Invitation Letter
: An official confirmation letter from the conference organizers in Italy.
Proof of travel insurance (minimum coverage of â¬30,000 for medical expenses and repatriation in the Schengen Area).
Proof of accommodation in Italy (hotel booking or declaration of hospitality).
Round-trip flight reservation/itinerary.
Financial proof (recent bank statements or evidence of sponsorship) to show sufficient funds for the stay.
Visa Application Process
:
Submit your application at the nearest Italian Embassy, Consulate, or authorized visa service center (such as VFS Global or CKGS) in your country.
Book an appointment well in advance, as slots can be limited during peak seasons.
Attend the interview (if required) and provide biometric data (fingerprints).
Pay the visa application fee (standard fee is â¬90, though exemptions may apply for certain categories).
Processing Time
: It typically takes about 15 calendar days to process an Italian Schengen visa. However, we strongly recommend applying at least
4â6 weeks
before your intended travel date to account for any delays.
Important Notes
:
If Italy is your main destination or the first point of entry into the Schengen Area, you must apply through the Italian diplomatic mission.
Ensure all documents are in English or Italian (or accompanied by a certified translation if required by the specific consulate).
The consular office reserves the right to request additional documentation or a personal interview.

Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy

Welcome to ICCMA 2026
æ¨çæµè§å¨ä¸æ¯æè§é¢æ ç­¾ã
ICCMA2025-Review
Control, Mechatronics and Automation is an interdisciplinary field that combines control engineering, mechanical engineering, and electronic engineering. In recent years, advancements in artificial intelligence, internet of things (IoT), and big data analytics have been driving the development of this field. Control, Mechatronics and Automation has become increasingly important in modern manufacturing and production systems, as well as in robotics, aerospace, transportation, and medical industries. The field is rapidly evolving with new advances in sensors, actuators, control algorithms, and communication technologies.
Since its inception in 2013, ICCMA has served as a key platform for international academic exchange. Previous editions of the conference have been successfully held in Sydney (Australia), Dubai (UAE), Barcelona (Spain), Edmonton (Canada), Tokyo (Japan), Moscow (Russia), and Luxembourg.In 2022, ICCMA was organized in a hybrid format, combining both on-site and online participation. The conference continued its global journey in 2023 in Norway, followed by ICCMA 2024 in London, UK. Most recently, the 13th ICCMA was held in Paris, France, in 2025, where it achieved great success and received widespread recognition from participants worldwide.
Based on the success of that, 2026 14th International Conference on Control, Mechatronics and Automation (ICCMA)
will be held at
Bari, Italy
, from
November 3-5, 2026
. CONFERENCE SCOPE
The conference aims to bring together 
										the two fields of system design and 
										control together. Today, flexible 
										robotics and compliant mechanism design 
										use nonlinearities which were once 
										considered hindrances to system design 
										to their advantage. In addition, 
										advances in control technology such as 
										nonlinear control, fractional order 
										control are advantageously being used to 
										go beyond classical linear control and 
										therefore increase the applicability of 
										complex mechanisms for robotics and 
										mechatronic systems. In this regard, the

ICCMA2025-Review
Control, Mechatronics and Automation is an interdisciplinary field that combines control engineering, mechanical engineering, and electronic engineering. In recent years, advancements in artificial intelligence, internet of things (IoT), and big data analytics have been driving the development of this field. Control, Mechatronics and Automation has become increasingly important in modern manufacturing and production systems, as well as in robotics, aerospace, transportation, and medical industries. The field is rapidly evolving with new advances in sensors, actuators, control algorithms, and communication technologies.
Since its inception in 2013, ICCMA has served as a key platform for international academic exchange. Previous editions of the conference have been successfully held in Sydney (Australia), Dubai (UAE), Barcelona (Spain), Edmonton (Canada), Tokyo (Japan), Moscow (Russia), and Luxembourg.In 2022, ICCMA was organized in a hybrid format, combining both on-site and online participation. The conference continued its global journey in 2023 in Norway, followed by ICCMA 2024 in London, UK. Most recently, the 13th ICCMA was held in Paris, France, in 2025, where it achieved great success and received widespread recognition from participants worldwide.
Based on the success of that, 2026 14th International Conference on Control, Mechatronics and Automation (ICCMA)
will be held at
Bari, Italy
, from
November 3-5, 2026
. It provides a platform for researchers, scholars, engineers, and students from around the world to present their research, share ideas, and foster research collaborations between academia and industry. ICCMA is a well-known event worldwide and has been attracting increasing numbers of paper submissions and attendees every year.

Link: Keynote Speakers
Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
Keynote Speakers
Prof. Maria Pia Fanti, IEEE FELLOW
Polytechnic of Bari, Italy
Maria Pia Fanti is full professor of System and Control Engineering at the Department of Electrical and Information Engineering of the Polytechnic of Bari (Italy). She received the Laurea degree in Electronic Engineering from the University of Pisa (Italy), in 1983. She was a visiting researcher at the Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute of Troy, New York, in 1999. Since 1983 she has been with the Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering of the Polytechnic of Bari (Italy), where she was Assistant Professor from 1990 till 1998 and Associate Professor from 1990 till April 2012.
Speech Title: Artificial Intelligence Approaches for Cooperative, Connected and Automated Mobility
Abstract: Cooperative, Connected and Automated Mobility (CCAM) is expected to reshape the way of travelling and moving in Europe and around the world. By the CCAM, the automated vehicles have to be integrated into the mobility and transport system by designing and implementing infrastructures, new services, platforms, cooperation and governance models.
Prof. Shane Xie, IEEE Fellow
University of Leeds, UK
Prof Shane (Sheng Q) Xie, Ph.D., FRSNZ, FEngNZ, FIEEE, FASME, FIMechE and FAAIA, is the Chair of Robotics and Autonomous Systems and Director of the Rehabilitation Robotics Lab at the University of Leeds. He has >30 years of research experience in healthcare robotics and exoskeletons. He has published > 500 refereed papers and 8 books in rehabilitation exoskeleton design and control, neuromuscular modelling, and advanced human-robot interaction.
Prof. Jing Zhou
University of Agder, Norway
Jing Zhou is a Professor at the Faculty of Engineering and Science, University of Agder, Norway. She is the leader of Robotics and Automation group, and the research director of Priority Research Center of Mechatronics. Prior to joining the University of Agder in 2016, she was a senior research scientist at International Research Institute of Stavanger in Norway from 2009 to 2016.

Link: ICCMA2025
Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA 2025 | NOV. 24-26 | 
								University of Versailles, France
The 13th International Conference on Control, Mechatronics and Automation (ICCMA 2025) was successfully held in Paris, France, at the University of Versailles. The conference attracted more than 150 participants from over 30 countries and regions, including Europe, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East, highlighting ICCMAâs strong international appeal and growing global influence.
The technical program of ICCMA 2025 was carefully structured to reflect current research trends and emerging challenges. The conference featured two main technical tracks, nine parallel oral sessions, two poster sessions, and dedicated online presentation sessions, ensuring broad participation and flexibility. In total, 110 high-quality papers were accepted and presented, covering a wide range of topics from intelligent control and robotics to automation systems and cyber-physical technologies. In addition, eight distinguished keynote and invited speakers delivered insightful talks, providing valuable perspectives on the future development of the field and stimulating active discussions among participants.
The opening session was chaired by Prof. Amar Ramdane-Cherif (University of Versailles Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines, France). The conference officially commenced with opening remarks by Prof. Laurent Dumas (University of Versailles, France), followed by a welcome address delivered by Prof. Belkacem Ould Bouamama (CRIStAL Laboratory, Polytech Lille, France), setting a strong academic tone for the event.
The plenary program featured two distinguished keynote speeches. Prof. Maria Pia Fanti, IEEE Fellow (Polytechnic University of Bari, Italy). Prof. Jing Zhou (University of Agder, Norway) delivered the second keynote . In addition, several invited talks further enriched the technical content of the conference. Prof. Roland BÃ¼chi (Zurich University of Applied Sciences, Switzerland) , Prof. Saulo H. Freitas Seabra da Rocha (Ruhr West University of Applied Sciences, Germany) . Prof. Jan DÃ¼nnweber (Ostbayerische Technische Hochschule Regensburg, Germany), Prof. Stijn Derammelaere (University of Antwerp, Belgium), and Assoc. Prof. Lorenzo Scalera (University of Udine, Italy) .
ICCMA 2025 was co-sponsored by the University of Versailles and the University of Lille. The organizers sincerely acknowledge their generous support and excellent local arrangements. Appreciation is also extended to the organizing committee, technical committee members, reviewers, and volunteers for their professionalism and dedication, which were essential to the success of the conference.

=== PAGE: https://www.iccma.org/faqs.html ===
Link: FAQs
Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
Frequently Asked 
										Questions
How to submit?
1. You need to edit your paper as instructed by 
								the template.
2. Send your paper to the
Easy 
								Chair System
.
3. After receiving your submission, the 
								conference secretary will contact you within 2 
								workdays and assign you a PAPER ID.
4. If 
								paper publication is your goal, then you can 
								submit full paper directly, no need to submit 
								abstract separately.
How long 
									is the paper review?
For each paper, it will take around 20-30 days 
								for review. You will receive feedback email.
1) For Paper receiving (2-3 workdays after your 
								successful submission)
2) Preliminary feedback (around 10-15 days after 
								your successful submission)
3) Acceptance letter notification (around 10-15 
								days after preliminary feedback)
What is the acceptance rate?
Each paper will be reviewed based on template, 
								topic, novelty, introduction, conceptual 
								quality, methodological quality, results, 
								discussion, readability, etc. The acceptance rate for our conference typically depends on the number and quality of submitted papers. The exact rate may vary from year to year, and we strive to maintain a high standard of quality for the presentations and discussions. We encourage authors to submit their best work, as the review process is highly competitive.
How to know whether my paper topic related to 
									the conference theme or not?
Please check the call for paper page:
http://iccma.org/topics.html
If your research topic is included here, it will 
								be good. If not, you also can submit your paper. 
								The technical committee will review the paper 
								topic firstly,you will know if your paper topics 
								related or not. Generally, you can know the 
								result within one week, even it is not related 
								to our conference, we can recommend you some 
								other related conferences.
How to make registration and how much should I pay?
Firstly, you need to figure out your 
								registration type: author (full paper 
								submission), presentation only (abstract 
								submission), listener (without paper or abstract 
								submission). Then you can check the detail 
								registration fee on our registration page:
http://iccma.org/reg.html
Is it necessary to register after submission?
You need to register after receiving the 
								notification of acceptance. We will inform you 
								the registration information by email when your 
								paper passes the review process.

Link: Organizer
Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
Organizer
The Polytechnic University of Bari is one of the Italian universities with a scientific-technological character recognized as a center of excellence for education and research in the fields of engineering, architecture, and industrial design. Poliba stands out for its strong connection with the territory and its commitment to sustainable development and technological innovation. The university promotes a teaching approach oriented towards practice and collaboration with local, national, and international businesses and institutions.
It trains, according to high-quality standards, professionals in the fields of engineering, architecture, design, and territorial planning, awarding academic degrees of university diploma and degree (old system, being phased out), bachelor's degree, master's degree, doctoral degree, with the corresponding academic qualifications, as well as first and second level university master's degrees.
The University of Lille (French: UniversitÃ© de Lille, abbreviated as ULille, UDL or univ-lille) is a French public research university based in Lille, Hauts-de-France. It has its origins in the University of Douai (1559), and resulted from the merger of three universities â Lille 1 University of Science and Technology, Lille 2 University of Health and Law, and Charles de Gaulle University â Lille III in 2018. With more than 80,000 students, it is one of the largest universities in France and one of the largest French-speaking universities in the world.
Since 2017, the university has been funded as one of the French universities of excellence. It benefits from an endowment of 500 million euros to accelerate its strategy in education, research, international development and outreach.
With 66 research labs, 350 PhD theses supported per year and 3,000 scientific publications each year, it is well represented in the research community; it collaborates with many organizations (Pasteur Institute of Lille, CHU Lille University Hospital, CNRS, INSERM, INRA, INRIA etc.) and schools (Ãcole Centrale de Lille, Ãcole des Mines-TÃ©lÃ©com de Lille-Douai (IMT Lille Douai), Sciences Po Lille etc.).
Until 2019, the university was the main component of the Community of Universities and Institutions (COMUE) Lille Nord de France. It still operates the European Doctoral College, which federates universities and other higher learning institutes in the Hauts-de-France region.
University of Versailles Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines (French: UniversitÃ© de Versailles Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines, pronounced is a French public university created in 1991, located in the department of Yvelines and, since 2002, in Hauts-de-Seine. It is a constituent university of the federal Paris-Saclay University.
Consisting of eight separate campuses, it is mainly located in the cities of Versailles, Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines, Mantes-en-Yvelines, VÃ©lizy-Villacoublay and Rambouillet. It is one of the five universities of the Academy of Versailles.[n 1]
It is one of the four universitÃ©s nouvelles (new universities)[n 2] inaugurated in the Ãle-de-France region after the 2000 University project (French: plan universitÃ© 2000). It has a population of 19,000 students, a staff of 752 people, and 1,389 teachers and researchers, as well as an additional 285 external teachers.
The university teaches courses in the fields of natural science, social science, political science, engineering, technology, and medicine. It also provides interdisciplinary courses covering the relationships across economics, ethics, natural environment and sustainable development.

It is our distinct pleasure to welcome you all to the 11th International Conference on Control, Mechatronics, and Automation. This remarkable gathering of minds from around the world, in the pursuit of advancing the fields of control, mechatronics, and automation, is a testament to the enduring spirit of innovation and collaboration that defines our global community.
In recent years, advancements in artificial intelligence, internet of things (IoT), and big data analytics have been driving the development of this field. Control, Mechatronics and Automation has become increasingly important in modern manufacturing and production systems, as well as in robotics, aerospace, transportation, and medical industries. The field is rapidly evolving with new advances in sensors, actuators, control algorithms, and communication technologies.
This conference is more than just a meeting of great minds; it is a nexus of inspiration, creativity, and collaboration. It's where ideas are conceived, knowledge is transferred, and partnerships are forged. The discussions and discoveries made here will undoubtedly chart the course for the future of these fields.

Link: Visa
Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
VISA
Schengen Visa Guidelines for Italy
For participants attending the conference from outside the Schengen Area, a Schengen visa may be required to enter Italy. Below are the general steps and requirements for applying for a Schengen visa:
Visa Type
: Participants attending the conference will need to apply for a
Short-Stay Schengen Visa
(Type C), which allows you to stay in Italy and other Schengen countries for up to 90 days for business or conference purposes.
Required Documents
:
A completed and signed visa application form.
A valid passport (with at least two blank pages and valid for at least three months beyond the planned departure date).
Recent passport-sized photographs (compliant with ICAO standards).
Invitation Letter
: An official confirmation letter from the conference organizers in Italy.
Proof of travel insurance (minimum coverage of â¬30,000 for medical expenses and repatriation in the Schengen Area).
Proof of accommodation in Italy (hotel booking or declaration of hospitality).
Round-trip flight reservation/itinerary.
Financial proof (recent bank statements or evidence of sponsorship) to show sufficient funds for the stay.
Visa Application Process
:
Submit your application at the nearest Italian Embassy, Consulate, or authorized visa service center (such as VFS Global or CKGS) in your country.
Book an appointment well in advance, as slots can be limited during peak seasons.
Attend the interview (if required) and provide biometric data (fingerprints).
Pay the visa application fee (standard fee is â¬90, though exemptions may apply for certain categories).
Processing Time
: It typically takes about 15 calendar days to process an Italian Schengen visa. However, we strongly recommend applying at least
4â6 weeks
before your intended travel date to account for any delays.
Important Notes
:
If Italy is your main destination or the first point of entry into the Schengen Area, you must apply through the Italian diplomatic mission.
Ensure all documents are in English or Italian (or accompanied by a certified translation if required by the specific consulate).
The consular office reserves the right to request additional documentation or a personal interview.
Copyright Â© 2026 The 14th 
					International Conference on Control, Mechatronics and 
					Automation (ICCMA 2026)

ICCMA2025-Review
Control, Mechatronics and Automation is an interdisciplinary field that combines control engineering, mechanical engineering, and electronic engineering. In recent years, advancements in artificial intelligence, internet of things (IoT), and big data analytics have been driving the development of this field. Control, Mechatronics and Automation has become increasingly important in modern manufacturing and production systems, as well as in robotics, aerospace, transportation, and medical industries. The field is rapidly evolving with new advances in sensors, actuators, control algorithms, and communication technologies.
Since its inception in 2013, ICCMA has served as a key platform for international academic exchange. Previous editions of the conference have been successfully held in Sydney (Australia), Dubai (UAE), Barcelona (Spain), Edmonton (Canada), Tokyo (Japan), Moscow (Russia), and Luxembourg.In 2022, ICCMA was organized in a hybrid format, combining both on-site and online participation. The conference continued its global journey in 2023 in Norway, followed by ICCMA 2024 in London, UK. Most recently, the 13th ICCMA was held in Paris, France, in 2025, where it achieved great success and received widespread recognition from participants worldwide.
Based on the success of that, 2026 14th International Conference on Control, Mechatronics and Automation (ICCMA)
will be held at
Bari, Italy
, from
November 3-5, 2026
. It provides a platform for researchers, scholars, engineers, and students from around the world to present their research, share ideas, and foster research collaborations between academia and industry. ICCMA is a well-known event worldwide and has been attracting increasing numbers of paper submissions and attendees every year.
The conference is sponsored by Polytechnic University of Bari, Italy.
Publication
Submitted papers will be peer reviewed and accepted papers after proper registration will be published in the
conference proceedings
.

5
has been successfully indexed by EI Compendex and Scopus already within 4 months after the conference.

>>Academic Ethics:
Academic integrity is the moral code or ethical policy of academia. This includes values such as avoidance of cheating or plagiarism; maintenance of academic standards; honesty and rigor in research and academic publishing. Each scholar should obey the rules. If not, the committee have the right to reject the paper directly when review the papers. Due to the update of database, but the publication process is long, if the press found the review paper is with plagiarism or content with high repetition rate, the press can reject the paper publication directly. The author will undertake all the responsibility.
Submission
The purpose of the Conference is to provide a common forum for researchers, scientists, and students from all over the world to present their latest research findings, ideas, developments and applications in the broader areas of Control, Mechatronics and Automation.
Please submit your full paper to
Easy Chair System
(.pdf only)
Please download the
Template(word)
|
Template(Latex)
to edit your paper submission before you submit your full paper.
The minimum page limit for full paper is
4
pages including all figures, tables, and references.
5
pages is the standard length. When it exceeds
6
pages, each additional page will be chargeable.
English is the official language; and the paper should be written and presented only in English.
Abstract will be accepted and author will be invited to give the oral presentation as well, however, neither the abstract nor the presentation would be published.
All submissions will undergo a double-blind peer review process.
Reviewers will evaluate the paper based on originality, technical quality, and relevance to the conference themes.
Special Sessions at ICCMA 2026
The 14th International Conference on Control, Mechatronics and Automation (ICCMA 2026) is proud to present a series of Special Sessions that highlight the latest advancements and emerging trends in the fields of control, mechatronics, robotics, and automation. These sessions are designed to foster collaboration and showcase cutting-edge research addressing the challenges and opportunities in todayâs rapidly evolving technological landscape. Welcome to join us to organize a special session which falls into your research area.
Special Session No. | Session Title (more to be updated, click for details...)
Session 1 | Nonlinear and Fractional-Order Control for Complex Mechatronic Systems | Organizer(s): TBA
Session 2 | Smart Automation and IoT-Enabled Industrial Process Control | Organizer(s): TBA
Session 3 | Machine Learning for Biomedical Instrumentation and Control | Organizer(s): TBA
Session 4 | Flexible Manufacturing and Additive Manufacturing for Industry 4.0 | Organizer(s): TBA
Session 5 | Vibration Control and Dynamics of Flexible Systems in Robotics | Organizer(s): TBA
Event History
ICCMA is initiated in Sydney (Australia) in 2013, followed by Dubai (UAE) in 2014 and the two consecutive editions of 2015 and 2016 in Barcelona (Spain), 2017 at University of Alberta, Edmonton, Canada, 2018 in Tokyo, Japan, 2019 in TU Delft, the Netherlands, 2020 in Moscow, Russia(Virtual), 2021 in Luxembourg(Virtual), and 2022 in Luxembourg (Hybrid), 2023 in University of Agder, Norway, 2024 in Brunel University London, UK, 2025 in University of Versailles, France. Link: Keynote Speakers
Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
Keynote Speakers
Prof. Maria Pia Fanti, IEEE FELLOW
Polytechnic of Bari, Italy
Maria Pia Fanti is full professor of System and Control Engineering at the Department of Electrical and Information Engineering of the Polytechnic of Bari (Italy). She received the Laurea degree in Electronic Engineering from the University of Pisa (Italy), in 1983. She was a visiting researcher at the Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute of Troy, New York, in 1999. Since 1983 she has been with the Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering of the Polytechnic of Bari (Italy), where she was Assistant Professor from 1990 till 1998 and Associate Professor from 1990 till April 2012.
Speech Title: Artificial Intelligence Approaches for Cooperative, Connected and Automated Mobility
Abstract: Cooperative, Connected and Automated Mobility (CCAM) is expected to reshape the way of travelling and moving in Europe and around the world. By the CCAM, the automated vehicles have to be integrated into the mobility and transport system by designing and implementing infrastructures, new services, platforms, cooperation and governance models.
Prof. Shane Xie, IEEE Fellow
University of Leeds, UK
Prof Shane (Sheng Q) Xie, Ph.D., FRSNZ, FEngNZ, FIEEE, FASME, FIMechE and FAAIA, is the Chair of Robotics and Autonomous Systems and Director of the Rehabilitation Robotics Lab at the University of Leeds. He has >30 years of research experience in healthcare robotics and exoskeletons. He has published > 500 refereed papers and 8 books in rehabilitation exoskeleton design and control, neuromuscular modelling, and advanced human-robot interaction.
Prof. Jing Zhou
University of Agder, Norway
Jing Zhou is a Professor at the Faculty of Engineering and Science, University of Agder, Norway. She is the leader of Robotics and Automation group, and the research director of Priority Research Center of Mechatronics. Prior to joining the University of Agder in 2016, she was a senior research scientist at International Research Institute of Stavanger in Norway from 2009 to 2016.

ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
Organizer
The Polytechnic University of Bari is one of the Italian universities with a scientific-technological character recognized as a center of excellence for education and research in the fields of engineering, architecture, and industrial design. Poliba stands out for its strong connection with the territory and its commitment to sustainable development and technological innovation. The university promotes a teaching approach oriented towards practice and collaboration with local, national, and international businesses and institutions.
It trains, according to high-quality standards, professionals in the fields of engineering, architecture, design, and territorial planning, awarding academic degrees of university diploma and degree (old system, being phased out), bachelor's degree, master's degree, doctoral degree, with the corresponding academic qualifications, as well as first and second level university master's degrees.
The University of Lille (French: UniversitÃ© de Lille, abbreviated as ULille, UDL or univ-lille) is a French public research university based in Lille, Hauts-de-France. It has its origins in the University of Douai (1559), and resulted from the merger of three universities â Lille 1 University of Science and Technology, Lille 2 University of Health and Law, and Charles de Gaulle University â Lille III in 2018. With more than 80,000 students, it is one of the largest universities in France and one of the largest French-speaking universities in the world.
Since 2017, the university has been funded as one of the French universities of excellence. It benefits from an endowment of 500 million euros to accelerate its strategy in education, research, international development and outreach.
With 66 research labs, 350 PhD theses supported per year and 3,000 scientific publications each year, it is well represented in the research community; it collaborates with many organizations (Pasteur Institute of Lille, CHU Lille University Hospital, CNRS, INSERM, INRA, INRIA etc.) and schools (Ãcole Centrale de Lille, Ãcole des Mines-TÃ©lÃ©com de Lille-Douai (IMT Lille Douai), Sciences Po Lille etc.).
Until 2019, the university was the main component of the Community of Universities and Institutions (COMUE) Lille Nord de France. It still operates the European Doctoral College, which federates universities and other higher learning institutes in the Hauts-de-France region.
University of Versailles Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines (French: UniversitÃ© de Versailles Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines, pronounced is a French public university created in 1991, located in the department of Yvelines and, since 2002, in Hauts-de-Seine. It is a constituent university of the federal Paris-Saclay University.
Consisting of eight separate campuses, it is mainly located in the cities of Versailles, Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines, Mantes-en-Yvelines, VÃ©lizy-Villacoublay and Rambouillet. It is one of the five universities of the Academy of Versailles.[n 1]
It is one of the four universitÃ©s nouvelles (new universities)[n 2] inaugurated in the Ãle-de-France region after the 2000 University project (French: plan universitÃ© 2000). It has a population of 19,000 students, a staff of 752 people, and 1,389 teachers and researchers, as well as an additional 285 external teachers.
The university teaches courses in the fields of natural science, social science, political science, engineering, technology, and medicine. It also provides interdisciplinary courses covering the relationships across economics, ethics, natural environment and sustainable development.

CONFERENCE SCOPE
The conference aims to bring together 
										the two fields of system design and 
										control together. Today, flexible 
										robotics and compliant mechanism design 
										use nonlinearities which were once 
										considered hindrances to system design 
										to their advantage. In addition, 
										advances in control technology such as 
										nonlinear control, fractional order 
										control are advantageously being used to 
										go beyond classical linear control and 
										therefore increase the applicability of 
										complex mechanisms for robotics and 
										mechatronic systems. In this regard, the 
										14th International Conference in Control, 
										Mechatronics and Automation solicits

Link: ICCMA2025
Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA 2025 | NOV. 24-26 | 
								University of Versailles, France
The 13th International Conference on Control, Mechatronics and Automation (ICCMA 2025) was successfully held in Paris, France, at the University of Versailles. The conference attracted more than 150 participants from over 30 countries and regions, including Europe, Asia, the Americas, and the Middle East, highlighting ICCMAâs strong international appeal and growing global influence.
The technical program of ICCMA 2025 was carefully structured to reflect current research trends and emerging challenges. The conference featured two main technical tracks, nine parallel oral sessions, two poster sessions, and dedicated online presentation sessions, ensuring broad participation and flexibility. In total, 110 high-quality papers were accepted and presented, covering a wide range of topics from intelligent control and robotics to automation systems and cyber-physical technologies. In addition, eight distinguished keynote and invited speakers delivered insightful talks, providing valuable perspectives on the future development of the field and stimulating active discussions among participants.
The opening session was chaired by Prof. Amar Ramdane-Cherif (University of Versailles Saint-Quentin-en-Yvelines, France). The conference officially commenced with opening remarks by Prof. Laurent Dumas (University of Versailles, France), followed by a welcome address delivered by Prof. Belkacem Ould Bouamama (CRIStAL Laboratory, Polytech Lille, France), setting a strong academic tone for the event.
The plenary program featured two distinguished keynote speeches. Prof. Maria Pia Fanti, IEEE Fellow (Polytechnic University of Bari, Italy). Prof. Jing Zhou (University of Agder, Norway) delivered the second keynote . In addition, several invited talks further enriched the technical content of the conference. Prof. Roland BÃ¼chi (Zurich University of Applied Sciences, Switzerland) , Prof. Saulo H. Freitas Seabra da Rocha (Ruhr West University of Applied Sciences, Germany) . Prof. Jan DÃ¼nnweber (Ostbayerische Technische Hochschule Regensburg, Germany), Prof. Stijn Derammelaere (University of Antwerp, Belgium), and Assoc. Prof. Lorenzo Scalera (University of Udine, Italy) .
ICCMA 2025 was co-sponsored by the University of Versailles and the University of Lille. The organizers sincerely acknowledge their generous support and excellent local arrangements. Appreciation is also extended to the organizing committee, technical committee members, reviewers, and volunteers for their professionalism and dedication, which were essential to the success of the conference.

It is our distinct pleasure to welcome you all to the 11th International Conference on Control, Mechatronics, and Automation. This remarkable gathering of minds from around the world, in the pursuit of advancing the fields of control, mechatronics, and automation, is a testament to the enduring spirit of innovation and collaboration that defines our global community.
In recent years, advancements in artificial intelligence, internet of things (IoT), and big data analytics have been driving the development of this field. Control, Mechatronics and Automation has become increasingly important in modern manufacturing and production systems, as well as in robotics, aerospace, transportation, and medical industries. The field is rapidly evolving with new advances in sensors, actuators, control algorithms, and communication technologies.
This conference is more than just a meeting of great minds; it is a nexus of inspiration, creativity, and collaboration. It's where ideas are conceived, knowledge is transferred, and partnerships are forged. The discussions and discoveries made here will undoubtedly chart the course for the future of these fields.

Link: FAQs
Title: ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
ICCMA2026|Bari, Italy
Frequently Asked 
										Questions
How to submit?
1. You need to edit your paper as instructed by 
								the template.
2. Send your paper to the
Easy 
								Chair System
.
3. After receiving your submission, the 
								conference secretary will contact you within 2 
								workdays and assign you a PAPER ID.
4. If 
								paper publication is your goal, then you can 
								submit full paper directly, no need to submit 
								abstract separately.
How long 
									is the paper review?
For each paper, it will take around 20-30 days 
								for review. You will receive feedback email.
1) For Paper receiving (2-3 workdays after your 
								successful submission)
2) Preliminary feedback (around 10-15 days after 
								your successful submission)
3) Acceptance letter notification (around 10-15 
								days after preliminary feedback)
What is the acceptance rate?
Each paper will be reviewed based on template, 
								topic, novelty, introduction, conceptual 
								quality, methodological quality, results, 
								discussion, readability, etc. The acceptance rate for our conference typically depends on the number and quality of submitted papers. The exact rate may vary from year to year, and we strive to maintain a high standard of quality for the presentations and discussions. We encourage authors to submit their best work, as the review process is highly competitive.
How to know whether my paper topic related to 
									the conference theme or not?
Please check the call for paper page:
http://iccma.org/topics.html
If your research topic is included here, it will 
								be good. If not, you also can submit your paper. 
								The technical committee will review the paper 
								topic firstly,you will know if your paper topics 
								related or not. Generally, you can know the 
								result within one week, even it is not related 
								to our conference, we can recommend you some 
								other related conferences.
How to make registration and how much should I pay?
Firstly, you need to figure out your 
								registration type: author (full paper 
								submission), presentation only (abstract 
								submission), listener (without paper or abstract 
								submission). Then you can check the detail 
								registration fee on our registration page:
http://iccma.org/reg.html
Is it necessary to register after submission?
You need to register after receiving the 
								notification of acceptance. We will inform you 
								the registration information by email when your 
								paper passes the review process.
